title: T-110 — bundle full NIST top-25K common-password list (replacing ~200 stub) (FT-050)
prompt:
# T-110 — bundle full NIST top-25K common-password list (replacing ~200 stub) (FT-050)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §13
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-050** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (S effort)

## Goal
packages/auth ships the full NIST top-25K list and a reproducible fetcher.

## Implementation contract
1. fetch-nist-25k.ts pulls from a documented source (commit-pinned), normalises (lowercase, dedupe), writes to data/common-passwords-25k.txt.
2. password-policy.ts loads the file once at module init.
3. Tests assert: (a) file has ≥ 24_000 entries; (b) 'password' → too_common; (c) 'TG7v$Bk2#xQ8nMz3' → not too_common.

## Files (scope)
- packages/auth/data/common-passwords-25k.txt [create]
- packages/auth/src/password-policy.ts [modify]
- packages/auth/src/__tests__/common-passwords-25k.test.ts [create]
- packages/auth/scripts/fetch-nist-25k.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the data file, when read, then it has at least 24_000 unique entries.
2. Given password='password', when validateNewPassword runs, then reasons includes 'too_common'.
3. Given a known-good strong password, when validateNewPassword runs, then 'too_common' is NOT in reasons.
4. Given the fetcher, when run twice, then the output file is byte-identical (deterministic).

## Test strategy
- RED: write common-passwords-25k.test.ts asserting entry count + behavior before list is bundled.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/auth test common-passwords-25k`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not commit a copyrighted list source.
- Do not load the file synchronously on every validate call (cache at init).
- Do not skip the deterministic-build assertion.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Replace the ~200-entry common-password stub with the full NIST top-25K list and add a fetcher script for reproducible regeneration.
